US20260042960A1.title: RED PHOSPHOR, PREPARATION METHOD THEREFOR, AND USE THEREOF
US20260042960A1.abstract: The present application discloses a red phosphor, and a preparation method and an application thereof and relates to the field of luminescent material technology. The chemical composition of the red phosphor is A2M(1-x)F6:xMn4+. The A is selected from at least one of alkali metal elements, and the M is selected from at least one of IVA group elements or a Ti element and a value range of the x is 0<x≤0.05. The red phosphor is granular; and a concentration of tetravalent manganese ions in a radial direction from a center of a particle of the red phosphor to a surface of the particle of the red phosphor decreases gradually. In the present application, the concentration of the activator Mn4+ in a radial direction from the center of the particle of the red phosphor to its surface is set to a descending gradient distribution to buffer the phosphor powder from the erosion of water vapor, thereby improving its anti-aging performance and prolonging the service life.

MX2025008752A.abstract: Described herein are multilayer moisture management assemblies for reversible absorption of water vapor where the assemblies regulate relative humidity in microclimates proximate the assemblies. The moisture management assemblies absorb water vapor from air when the relative humidity level in the air is at or above a threshold level, thus retarding further elevation of the relative humidity. Articles incorporating the assemblies are also disclosed.

MX2025007225A.title: PROCESO Y COMPOSICION PARA LA PRODUCCION DE CONCRETO POLIMERICO UTILIZANDO AGUA CON SAL COMO MEDIO DE REACCION.
MX2025007225A.abstract: The present invention relates to an innovative process and composition for the manufacture of polymeric concrete using a salt water solution as a reaction medium. This approach replaces the glycols derived from fossil fuels, achieving a significant reduction in production costs between 30% and 40% and promoting environmental sustainability by generating water vapor as a reusable byproduct and allowing the recovery of up to 90% of the salt used. The composition includes an unsaturated polyester resin obtained from recycled PET bottles, styrene monomer, granulates and recycled sand, in addition to advanced additives such as graphene oxide, carbon black, and calcium carbonate, which together optimize the mechanical properties of the concrete. The production process incorporates ultrasonic dispersion technologies to ensure a homogeneous mixture and efficient integration of nanometer materials, improving tensile strength, compression, and chemical agents. This polymeric concrete is characterized by its versatility and sustainability, being applicable in sectors such as road infrastructure, sustainable construction, industrial environments, coastal structures and advanced architectures. In addition, the formulation thereof allows applications in paints, waterproofing agents, composite materials, decorative moulds, repairs, electronics, synthetic fibres, 3D printing and consumer goods, highlighting the adaptability thereof to different technical and industrial needs.

MX2025002635A.title: PAVING MEMBRANE
MX2025002635A.abstract: A paving membrane (1) includes a flexible plastic layer impermeable to water and water vapor (4) of the film-like plastic having a top side (5) and a bottom side (6) and an anchor layer (11) firmly attached to the bottom side (6) of the plastic layer (4). The plastic layer includes a plurality of buttons (7) present on the top side, each of the buttons forming a button cavity (8) on the bottom side. Water or water vapour discharge channels (9) are formed on the underside of the plastic layer (4). WO2026029165A1.abstract: The present invention provides: a vapor-deposited multilayer film which has high oxygen barrier properties and high water vapor barrier properties, and has sufficient oxygen barrier properties even after a bending treatment; and the like. Provided is a vapor-deposited multilayer film comprising: a multilayer film in which a layer (A), as the outermost layer, a layer (B), and a layer (C) are stacked in this order; and a vapor-deposited inorganic layer (I) stacked on the exposed surface side of the layer (A). The average thickness of the multilayer film is 300 µm or less.

US20260026974A1.title: MULTI-FUNCTIONAL ANALGESIC-RELEASING WOUND DRESSING
US20260026974A1.abstract: A wound dressing including a porous biosynthetic polymer film comprising a poly(lactide-co-caprolactone) (“PLC”) copolymer or other suitable polymer matrix, and a local analgesic loaded therein, to provide pain relief over an extended period of time (e.g., for 5-7 days), e.g., for healing of a partial thickness dermal injury. The PLC copolymer may have a particular lactide/caprolactone molar ratio (e.g., from 2:1 to 9:1) to ensure slow, sustained release of the bupivacaine or other analgesic. The dressing may provide a water vapor transmission rate from 1000 g/m2·day to 3000 g/m2·day to facilitate fast healing and regrowth of new epithelial tissue beneath the dressing. The dressing is configured to remain positioned over the dermal injury, providing wound protection for about 14 days, after which is simply debrides away, falls off, or is easily and painlessly removed, once new epithelial tissue regrows.

AU2025205300A1.title: Atmospheric correction
AU2025205300A1.abstract: This disclosure relates to machine learning models for performing atmospheric correction on an input image. To train a machine learning model to perform atmospheric correction on an input image comprising atmospheric distortion, a processor applies a first trained machine learning model to a training image to determine a first output image, the first trained machine learning model being configured to perform atmospheric correction on an input image comprising atmospheric distortion. The processor applies a second machine learning model to the training image to determine a second output image, wherein the second machine learning model has a smaller model architecture than the first trained machine learning model. The processor trains the second machine learning model by minimising a loss based on the first output image and the second output image to determine a second trained machine learning model. KR102907001B1.abstract: 본 발명에서는 기존 산업용 보일러 배기가스내 수증기의 경우에, 백연 발생, 오염물질 확산 방행, 2차 입자상 물질 생성 촉진, 에어로졸을 활성화시키는 문제점과, 보일러 배기가스의 경우에, 대기 중에 냉각되면서 응축시 미세먼지(PM2.5)를 생성시키는데, 이는 지름이 2.5㎛(마이크로미터, 1㎛=100만분의 1m) 이하인 초미세먼지로서, 크기가 작아 호흡기를 통해 폐와 혈관까지 침투하여 건강에 심각한 영향을 미치기 때문에, 탄소배출권 시장에서 권고하고, 미국 EPA (μg/m³) 기준 연평균 12 μg/m³ 이하로 생성시킬 수 있는 보일러 배기가스용 미세먼지 저감장치개발이 시급한 문제점을 개선하고자, 하이브리드형 배기열 회수 모듈(100), 저온 배기열 회수 흡수탑모듈(200), 스마트 증류탑 순환루프 형성모듈(300), 시상수·응축수 탱크부(400), 스마트제어부(500)로 구성됨으로서, 하이브리드형 배기열 회수 모듈을 통해, 현열과 잠열을 동시 회수하는 하이브리드 방식으로 적용하여, 기존보다 30~50% 이상의 배기열 회수 효율을 향상시킬 수 있고, 배기열 회수량이 증가함에 따라 보일러 연료 소모량을 20% 이상 절감시킬 수 있으며, 난방뿐만 아니라 냉방, 온수 공급까지 동시에 가능하여, 에너지원 활용의 범위를 넓힐 수 있고, 배기 가스 중 포함된 미세먼지(입자상물질, PM)와 수증기 응축이 용이하여, 탄소배출권 시장에도 미세먼지 저감장치로서 경쟁력있게 적용시킬 수 있고, 저온 배기열 회수 흡수탑모듈이 구성됨으로서, 하이브리드형 배기열 회수 모듈과 연계하여 현열 + 잠열을 동시에 회수하는 방식을 적용함으로써, 기존보다 낮은 온도 20~40℃ 에서도 배기열을 회수시킬 수 있고, 흡수제 직접 분사로 노점온도 하강 및 배기열 손실 최소화시킬 수 있으며, 4단형 흡수탑 구조로 흡수 효율을 기존보다 80% 향상시킬 수 있고, 기존에는 흡수제의 재사용이 어려워 운영 비용이 높았으나, 본 발명에서는 흡수제 재생 시스템을 포함하여 흡수제의 지속적인 사용이 가능하므로 경제성이 뛰어난 효과가 있고, 스마트 증류탑 순환루프 형성모듈을 통해, 저온 배기열 회수 흡수탑모듈을 활용하여, 폐열을 회수하여 저농도 흡수제를 예열하고, 낮은 온도에서도 증발이 가능하도록 감압 분위기를 형성시켜, 기존 대비 기존 대비 30~50% 이상의 에너지 절감 효과를 기대할 수 있고, 감압 분위기 내에서 증발 촉진 및 증류 속도를 기존에 비해, 1.5배~2배 향상시킬 수 있으며, 농축 흡수제 냉각기와 농축 흡수제 가열기를 활용하여 적절한 온도로 순환시킴으로써, 흡수제의 성능 저하를 방지하고 흡수제의 사용 수명을 기존보다 1.5배~3배 길게 연장시킬 수 있고, 증발된 수증기의 응축수를 시상수·응축수 탱크부로 이동하여 재사용될 수 있어, 수자원 절약율을 기존에 비해 80% 향상시킬 수 있으며, 스마트제어부가 구성됨으로서, 하이브리드형 배기열 회수 제어모드를 통해, 배기가스의 현열(150~120°C)과 잠열(40~20°C)을 동시에 회수하여 실내 난방 및 온수 공급에 재활용시킬 수 있고,단순히 공기를 데우는 것이 아니라, 배기열을 최대한 활용하여 냉난방 시스템의 에너지 효율을 기존에 비해 80% 향상시킬 수 있으며, 산업용 가스보일러의 백연 제거와 동시에 미세먼지(여과성+응축성)를 80% 이상 제거시킬 수 있고, 보일러 배기가스에 포함된 수분 제거율을 80% 이상 제거시킬 수 있는 미세먼지 저감·수증기 증발·잠열 재활용 쓰리이펙트형 스마트 산업용 보일러 배기가스 순환루프 형성제어장치 및 방법을 제공하는데 그 목적이 있다.

AU2024295884A1.title: MATERIALS, MANUFACTURING METHOD AND DEVICE FOR CONVERTING AMBIENT HUMIDITY TO ELECTRICAL ENERGY AND USES THEREOF
AU2024295884A1.abstract: The present disclosure relates to a modular converter for continuous conversion of water vapor into electricity comprising: a porous matrix comprising compressed metal oxide nanopowder, wherein the porous matrix, is covered from first side by the functional layer, which partially may infuse in the porous matrix, preferably an aluminum phosphate; a layer of carbon electrode, preferably a layer of graphite on the active layer, metal or carbon current collectors on carbon electrode layer and layer of metal Au, Ag, Al, Cu, Fe, Zn, Mg, Li, Ni, Co, Ti, Zr, Sn, Cr, Y, Nb, Mo, Rd, Pt, Cd, Hf, Ta, W, C or their alloys. Schottky contact on the second side of the converter; and metal or carbon current collectors, on the layer of Schottky contact, wherein the second surface of the converter is directly opposite the first surface of the converter.

WO2026016265A1.title: REVÊTEMENT COMPOSITE HYDROPHOBE/RÉSISTANT À LA CORROSION RICHE EN CHROME, SON PROCÉDÉ DE PRÉPARATION ET SON UTILISATION
WO2026016265A1.abstract: A hydrophobic/chromium-rich corrosion-resistant composite coating, a preparation method therefor, and the use thereof, relating to the technical field of material surface coatings. The preparation method comprises: preheating a workpiece; applying a coating slurry to the surface of the preheated workpiece, and performing drying and curing and sintering treatment to obtain a chromium-rich corrosion-resistant coating on the surface of the workpiece, the coating slurry comprising a solid-phase component and a liquid-phase component, wherein the solid-phase component comprises chromium powder, nickel powder, iron powder and a rare earth compound, and the liquid-phase component comprises Al(H2PO4)3, water glass, an ammonium halide, polyvinyl alcohol and Cr2O3; and spray-coating a layer of a superhydrophobic material on the surface of the chromium-rich corrosion-resistant coating, and then performing curing treatment to obtain the hydrophobic/chromium-rich corrosion-resistant composite coating. A workpiece undergoes chromizing treatment and superhydrophobic treatment and thus possesses corrosion resistance, wear resistance and hydrophobic properties, so that water vapor in tubes cannot stay on the inner walls of the tubes, thereby preventing corrosion of the inner walls of the tubes, improving the comprehensive performance of the tubes, and prolonging the service life.

WO2026020150A1.title: SYSTÈMES ET PROCÉDÉS DE DÉSHYDRATANT THERMIQUE SOLAIRE À HAUT RENDEMENT
WO2026020150A1.abstract: A water generation system includes a housing having a surface to collect solar radiation; a sorption layer comprising a hygroscopic material to capture water vapor from a process gas during a sorption mode and release water vapor to a regeneration fluid during a desorption mode; and a thermally insulative transparent layer disposed above or upstream of the hygroscopic material to allow solar radiation to pass therethrough to the hygroscopic material and reduce thermal conductance from the hygroscopic material to the external environment. Generating water includes allowing solar radiation to pass through a thermally insulative transparent layer to a hygroscopic material; directing a regeneration fluid in a regeneration flow path to collect heat from the thermally insulative transparent layer and transfer collected heat to the hygroscopic material to release water vapor to the regeneration fluid; and condensing, via a heat exchange assembly, water vapor from the regeneration fluid.

US12523035B1.title: Water vapor permeable ribbed membranes and related systems and related methods
US12523035B1.abstract: Water vapor permeable ribbed membranes and related methods are provided. A water vapor permeable ribbed membrane has a top surface and a bottom surface opposite the top surface. The water vapor permeable ribbed membrane comprises a plurality of ribs located on at least the bottom surface. An adhesive layer is located on the plurality of ribs. When the adhesive layer is adhered to a top surface of another water vapor permeable ribbed membrane, the adhesive layer has a peel adhesion of at least 1.5 lb/in, as measured according to ASTM D3330 (Method B). The water vapor permeable ribbed membrane has a water vapor permeability of greater than 10 perms, as measured according to ASTM E96B.

US20260022021A1.abstract: Carbonaceous substance-coated graphite particles are disclosed that can be used as a negative electrode material for a lithium ion secondary battery. The carbonaceous substance-coated graphite particles include graphite particles and a carbonaceous substance covering at least part of surfaces of the graphite particles. A mass reduction starting temperature when the carbonaceous substance-coated graphite particles are heated in a water vapor atmosphere is 800° C. to 980° C., and a content of the carbonaceous substance is 0.1 to 15.0 parts by mass with respect to 100.0 parts by mass of the graphite particles.

GEAP201814569A.title: DEVICE FOR SYNTHETIC GAS PRODUCTION
GEAP201814569A.abstract: Synthetic gas production device includes a bin 1 and heating section 2, which is disposed in combustion chamber and has diffusive part, which restricting area is directed to mixing chamber 7 and connected therewith passing pipe with diameter equal of said restricting area diameter. Mixing chamber 7 is executed in a form of truncated cone and disposed above gasification section. Raw bin is connected to mixing chamber with auger feeder 4. Water vapor feeding section 6 is disposed above gasification section, in mixing chamber area so, that together with raw mix and heat carrier feeding into gasification section the vapor feeding would be provided directly to said mix stream motion. Gasification section has diffusion part, as well, which restricting area is connected to a regenerator 8, having diffusive part connected to delivery mouth 10. Productional gas outlet means is connected to gasification section; furthermore, a device is equipped with a transporter 12 carring outgoing heat carrier-catalyst from device inner capacity through a lattice 11 into receving bin 1.

US20260015836A1.title: ATMOSPHERIC WATER GENERATOR SUPPLIED WITH PHOTOVOLTAIC SOLAR POWER
US20260015836A1.abstract: A photovoltaic device, comprising: a photovoltaic generator whose electrical production power varies; an air humidifier device comprising: a storage compartment for atmospheric water vapor; means for heating and injecting the ambient air into the storage compartment; means for cooling and extracting the air flow available at the outlet of the storage compartment; an atmospheric water vapor condenser, which draws in the air flow previously humidified and cooled by the air humidifier device; a management module of the electrical power produced by the photovoltaic generator and used to electrically power the air humidifier device and the condenser device; said means for cooling and extracting the air flow being supplied with a variable electrical power controlled by the value of the surplus of photovoltaic electrical power available, from when the electrical power produced by the photovoltaic generator is greater than said electrical power used by the condenser.

US20260016230A1.title: METHOD FOR SETTING AN OVEN ATMOSPHERE IN A HEAT-TREATMENT OVEN
US20260016230A1.abstract: A method of establishing a furnace atmosphere in a directly heated heat treatment furnace is provided. A heat treatment furnace has at least one burner which is operated with a fuel gas and an oxygenous gas that are combusted to give a combustion gas. The combustion gas has a defined composition having a defined partial water vapor pressure. Hydrogen is used in the fuel gas with a proportion of at least 10% by volume. The heat treatment furnace is additionally flooded with a water vapor-free and/or hydrogen-free gas, as a result of which the water vapor-free and/or hydrogen-free gas mixes with the combustion gas so as to bring about a partial water vapor pressure of the mixture in the furnace atmosphere of the heat treatment furnace that is less than the defined partial water vapor pressure of the combustion gas.

US20260015706A1.title: METHOD AND DEVICE FOR APPLYING A LAYER ONTO A FLAT STEEL PRODUCT
US20260015706A1.abstract: A device for applying a layer (to a front and/or rear side of a flat steel product includes a zinc alloy melt bath (11) (ZnAl; ZnAlMg) with an input side and an exit side, a stripping nozzle device with at least one gas nozzle for blowing off the front or rear side of the flat steel product with gas, the stripping nozzle device being arranged in the area of the exit side, and a water vapor device which is configured to emit gaseous water vapor and to provide a controlled water vapor atmosphere in the close range of the front and/or rear side of the flat steel product. The controlled water vapor atmosphere has an absolute local humidity which is greater than 1 g/m3 and less than 300 g/m3, the absolute local humidity preferably being in the range of 2.71 g/m3 to 50 g/m3.

US20260015705A1.title: DEVICE AND METHOD AND DEVICE FOR MOISTURE-CONTROLLED BLOW-OFF AFTER THE APPLICATION OF A LAYER ONTO A FLAT STEEL PRODUCT
US20260015705A1.abstract: A device and method for applying ZnAlMg layers or ZnAl layers to the front and rear side of a flat steel product, includes moving the flat steel product from an input side to an exit side of a zinc alloy melt bath, providing a dry gas stream, providing a water vapor gas, combining the dry gas stream and the water vapor gas to obtain a stripping gas as a mixture, determining the gas humidity of the stripping gas, discharging the stripping gas through at least one gas nozzle which serves to blow off the front side and through at least one gas nozzle which serves to blow off the back side in order to blow off the front side and rear side of the flat steel product with the stripping gas.

US20260008700A1.title: ADIABATIC WATER DESALINATION SYSTEM
US20260008700A1.abstract: Methods and systems are disclosed for easy and cost-effective adiabatic water desalination, producing freshwater and/or potable water from saline sources with minimal energy input. This is achieved by utilizing adiabatic thermodynamic processes to reduce energy consumption compared to conventional methods. The disclosed methods and systems offer sustainable and cost-effective solutions to address freshwater scarcity. In an example of these methods, a saltwater tank with two chambers is rocked back and forth around an axis such that, during each half cycle of rocking, the vapor volume of one chamber expands and the vapor volume of the other chamber shrinks. The expansion of the chambers' vapor volume vaporizes the salt water, and the shrinkage of the chambers' vapor volume causes condensation of the water vapor, which will be collected in a separate tank.

WO2026011792A1.title: MOULE D'ESTAMPAGE À CHAUD DE PETIT LOT BASÉ SUR UN REFROIDISSEMENT PAR L'AIR DIRECTEMENT SUR LA SURFACE , ET PROCÉDÉ DE REFROIDISSEMENT
WO2026011792A1.abstract: A small-batch hot stamping mold based on direct surface air cooling, comprising a male mold (20) and a female mold (30), wherein a recess matching a punch of the male mold is provided on the female mold, a first cavity (21) recessed toward the interior of the male mold is provided on a surface of the male mold, a second cavity (31) recessed toward the interior of the female mold is further provided on a surface of the female mold, and the first cavity and the second cavity are configured to form an airflow cooling channel in communication with an external pipe; and water vapor introduced through the external pipe enters the mold, and circulates and exchanges heat in the airflow cooling channel formed by the first cavity and the second cavity and on the surface of the mold, so as to take away the heat from a stamped part and the surface of the mold. Further disclosed are a cooling method for a stamped part based on direct surface air cooling and a cooling method for a stamping mold based on direct surface air cooling. The stamping mold is provided with the cavities on the surface of the mold, the manufacturing cost and maintenance cost of the mold are low, and the production cost of a single piece is reduced.

US20260008689A1.title: INSTALLATION AND METHOD FOR CONVERTING URANIUM HEXAFLUORIDE TO URANIUM DIOXIDE
US20260008689A1.abstract: An installation for the conversion of uranium hexafluoride (UF6) to uranium dioxide (UO2) comprises a hydrolysis reactor (4) for the conversion of UF6 into uranium oxyfluoride powder (UO2F2), a pyrohydrolysis furnace (6) for converting the UO2F2 powder supplied by the reactor (4) into UO2 powder, a supply device (8) comprising reagent injection ducts (10) for the injection of UF6, water vapor or H2, and a control system (16) designed to control the supply device (8) so as to supply at least one of the reagent injection ducts (10) with a neutral gas during a shut-down or start-up phase of the conversion installation.

US20260008695A1.title: METHOD FOR PRODUCING POSITIVE ELECTRODE ACTIVE MATERIAL
US20260008695A1.abstract: A method for producing a positive electrode active material comprising: (1) mixing an activation agent containing one or more types of alkali metal compounds with an electrode material mixture; (2) heating the mixture at a temperature at a melting start temperature of the activation agent or more; (3) bringing the mixture after step (2) into contact with a water-containing liquid to obtain a slurry, and separating the slurry into a solid component and a liquid component; (4) removing water from the solid component by subjecting the solid component to heating and/or a reduced-pressure environment, wherein an ambient pressure at which the solid component exists and/or a temperature thereof is changed so that a ratio of saturated water vapor pressure at the temperature thereof to the ambient pressure becomes 80% or more within 24 hours after slurry separation; and (5) heat-treating the solid component after step (4) at lower than 900° C.

US20260011742A1.abstract: An iron-based cathode lithium-supplementing material, a preparation method thereof, and an application thereof. The iron-based cathode lithium-supplementing material includes a lithium-rich iron-based core and a passivation layer coated on a surface of the lithium-rich iron-based core. A material of the passivation layer includes LiFeO2 and LidMOe, d and e satisfy d≥2e−5, and the M is selected from at least one element of Al, Ni, Mn, Co, Ti, Zr, Si, V, Zn, Cr, Cu, and P. LiFeO2 and LidMOe form a passivation layer on the surface of the lithium-rich iron-based core, and the passivation layer has high density and does not react with water, and can have effect in isolating harmful components, such as water and carbon dioxide, in the air, ensuring the effect of isolating the material from water vapor during storage, and has stable performance, which is conducive to widespread application.

WO2026009969A1.title: APPAREIL DE PRODUCTION D'HYDROGÈNE ET PROCÉDÉ DE PRODUCTION D'HYDROGÈNE
WO2026009969A1.abstract: The present invention addresses the problem of providing a hydrogen production apparatus using a solid oxide electrolysis cell, in which the water vapor utilization rate is high, and followability of a change in water vapor supply flow rate with respect to a power load fluctuation is good . The present invention also addresses the problem of providing a hydrogen production method using the hydrogen production apparatus.　A hydrogen production apparatus 10 has a reactor R in which a solid oxide type electrolysis cell 10 is installed, the solid oxide type electrolysis cell 10 including: a water vapor electrode 20 in which an electrolytic reaction of water vapor occurs; a gas-impermeable and ion-permeable solid oxide electrolyte 40; and a counter electrode 30 in which a reaction of a charge carrier that is generated through the electrolytic reaction in the water vapor electrode 20 and that passes through the solid oxide electrolyte 40 occurs. The hydrogen production apparatus 10 has an injector 23 that supplies water in a pulsed manner to the water vapor electrode 20 side of the reactor R. The above problems are solved by supplying water to the water vapor electrode 20 in a pulsed manner.

PL449167A1.title: Volatile organic compounds and water vapor sensor and method of manufacturing a sensor electrode matrix from Mₙ₊₁XₙTₓ type materials
PL449167A1.abstract: Przedmiotem zgłoszenia jest sensor lotnych związków organicznych oraz pary wodnej, a także sposób wytwarzania elektrod sensora z materiałów typu Mn+1XnTx. Sensor ten umożliwia detekcję, identyfikację i określenie stężeń lotnych związków organicznych w atmosferze powietrza, jak również gazów obojętnych przy różnym poziomie wilgotności względnej. Sensor posiada komorę sensorową połączoną z blokiem sterująco-odczytowym i z blokiem analizy. W komorze znajduje się element grzejny, na którym umieszczona jest matryca składającą się od 2 do 20 elektrod, posiadających podłoże z ceramiki Al2O3, na którym znajduje się warstwa materiału typu MXene, o grubości co najmniej 100 nm, domieszkowana jonami pierwiastków z grupy 4 - 12 układu okresowego. Sposób wykonania matrycy elektrod sensora polega na tym, że najpierw przygotowuje się za pomocą syntezy chemicznej materiał Mn+1XnTx, gdzie x wynosi 1, 2 albo 3. Następnie materiał ten nanosi się na 2 do 20 podłoży z ceramiki Al2O3 i suszy się. Później warstwę Mn+1XnTx znajdującą się na podłożach poddaje się implantacji jonami pierwiastków z grup 4 - 12 układu okresowego. Na zakończenie, na poszczególnych elektrodach matrycy wykonuje się kontakty elektryczne.

WO2026007011A1.title: SYSTÈME ET PROCÉDÉ DE MODIFICATION DE CARBONATATION EN SUSPENSION À BASSE PRESSION DE MATÉRIAU PULVÉRULENT COMBINÉ À UNE RÉDUCTION D'ÉMISSION DE GAZ DE COMBUSTION INDUSTRIEL CO2
WO2026007011A1.abstract: Disclosed in the present invention are a system and method for low-pressure suspension carbonation modification of a powdery material combined with emission reduction of industrial flue gas CO2, comprising a low-pressure suspension carbonation bed system, a bag dust collector, and an induced draft fan. The low-pressure suspension carbonation bed system comprises an air duct, a suspension carbonation bed, and a water spraying system. High-temperature and high-CO2 flue gas and powdery material are sprayed upward into the suspension carbonation bed, and water or an aqueous solution of a carbonation aid is sprayed into the suspension carbonation bed in the form of droplets, so that the water vapor in the flue gas forms a critical saturation state, forming a liquid film on the surface of the material. The CO2 in the flue gas dissolves into the liquid film to undergo a carbonation reaction with the material. The flue gas carries the material and enters the bag dust collector, where part of the collected powder returns to the low-pressure suspension carbonation bed system for cyclic carbonation, and part is used as a modified carbonated material. The present invention achieves sufficient carbonation of powdery material and flue gas in a suspended state, improves the carbonation rate, reduces the emission of CO2 in the flue gas, and is conducive to large-scale and continuous production of material carbonation processes.

TW202539912A.title: Optical film and display device
TW202539912A.abstract: This optical film includes: a base material; and a functional layer that contains a resin component and a functional component that is an antimicrobial agent and/or an antiviral agent. The functional component is an organic material. The proportion of the functional component relative to the resin component in the functional layer is 40 mass% or less. WO2026007818A1.title: DISPOSITIF DE BAIN DE PIED
WO2026007818A1.abstract: The present invention relates to a foot bath device. The massage assemblies of existing foot bath devices feature a poor user experience. The present invention comprises a housing provided with a foot bath cavity therein. A sauna assembly is disposed in the foot bath cavity. High-temperature water vapor generated by the sauna assembly diffuses in the foot bath cavity and surrounds the feet of a user. A massage assembly is disposed in the housing and extracts, pressurizes, and sprays the high-temperature water vapor to form fluid beams, and the fluid beams flush the feet to generate massage stimulation. The massage assembly is configured for forming massage stimulation by spraying the fluid beams. The massage assembly pressurizes and sprays the high-temperature water vapor to form fluid beams having kinetic energy and thermal energy, so that multiple massage stimulations, including pressing stimulation and thermal stimulation, can be applied to the feet by using the fluid beams, thereby improving massage comfort. In addition, the high-temperature water vapor in the foot bath cavity can also be cyclically heated for use, reducing water consumption and maintaining the temperature in the foot bath cavity and thereby improving the user experience.

TW202537702A.title: Substrate processing apparatus and substrate processing method
TW202537702A.abstract: The technology disclosed in the specification of the present application is technology for separating mixed vapor of IPA vapor and water vapor, which represents a problem unsolved in the prior art. A substrate treatment device relating to the technology disclosed in the specification of the present application comprises: a treatment chamber in which a treatment tank having pure water stored therein is accommodated, a substrate is immersed in the pure water, and the substrate is thereby treated; an organic solvent vapor supply unit that supplies vapor of an organic solvent into the treatment chamber; a lifter that pulls up the substrate while bringing the vapor of the organic solvent into contact with the substrate when the substrate immersed in the pure water is removed from the treatment tank; a suction unit that sucks in mixed vapor generated in the treatment chamber from within the treatment chamber; and a separation membrane that separates vapor of the pure water and vapor of the organic solvent in the mixed vapor sucked in by the suction unit. GB2642940A.title: Chemical coolant for aerosol free extinguishing agent and preparation method
GB2642940A.abstract: Disclosed are a chemical coolant for an aerosol fire extinguishing agent and a preparation method. The chemical coolant is prepared by means of selecting an inorganic salt of K/Na/Mg/Ca/Sr that can undergo a decomposition reaction and combining same with an organic flame retardant and a binder. By using a metal inorganic salt, a decomposition reaction occurs at a high temperature to generate active metal substances such as K/Na/Mg/Ca/Sr oxide, and the fire extinguishing capacity is enhanced while combustion heat is absorbed. The organic flame retardant also undergoes a vaporization or decomposition reaction at a high temperature to generate a non-combustible gas such as NH3, N2, CO2, and water vapor, the aerosol content is increased while flame extinction and cooling are achieved, and the fire extinguishing capacity is enhanced. Furthermore, the organic flame retardant also has certain binding characteristics. An auxiliary binder is used for binding the chemical coolant to increase the stability, thereby preventing the metal inorganic salt in the chemical coolant from experiencing pulverization during storage, transportation, and use.

US12508547B1.title: System for desalinating water with multiple desalination processes
US12508547B1.abstract: A system for water desalination includes a membrane module and an ejector module. The membrane module includes a feed tank for salt water, a heater, a humidifier receiving heated salt water and a carrier gas to form a wet gas, and a membrane distillation unit. The membrane distillation unit includes a feed chamber, a membrane, and a permeate chamber with a sweeping gas inlet. The membrane allows water vapor to pass from the feed chamber to the permeate chamber. The ejector module includes an ejector and a bubble column dehumidifier. The ejector has first and second inlets for primary and secondary fluid streams, respectively, creating a mixed stream. The bubble column dehumidifier receives the mixed stream to produce fresh water. The second inlet of the ejector receives both wet gas from the humidifier and sweeping gas from the permeate chamber as the secondary fluid stream.

UA161811U.title: CUTTING METHOD IN DIRECT POLARITY OF METAL SHEETS
UA161811U.abstract: Plasma cutting method in direct polarity of metal sheets with water usage involves supplying water to the hollow electrode of the plasma torch through capillary holes, with a water flow rate of 0.035...0.055 l/min, and the specific value of the water flow rate is determined depending on the thickness of the metal plate being cut and the capillary holes. The water evaporates under the action of heat, first from the auxiliary arc and then from the cutting arc, after which it mixes with the vortex of plasma-forming gas to form a gas mixture containing 20% water vapor. The auxiliary arc is ignited between the hollow electrode of the plasma torch and the electrode-nozzle, which are separated by an electrically insulated cyclone with tangential holes for supplying plasma-forming gas. The cutting arc is ignited between the hollow electrode of the plasma torch and the metal being cut. In this case, water is supplied along the axis of a hollow copper cathode electrode, which has a ring-shaped hafnium insert at its working end, with the water evaporation zone located outside the cathode spot zone inside the cavity of the copper cathode electrode, and its depth relative to the hafnium insert does not exceed 1-2 mm.

WO2026000350A1.title: SYSTÈME INTÉGRÉ POUR LA RÉCUPÉRATION D'AIR THERMIQUE RÉSIDUEL DE CNA ET DE CENTRE DE DONNÉES
WO2026000350A1.abstract: Provided is an integrated system for DAC and data center residual heat air recovery. The system comprises: a waste heat recovery and utilization unit, a carbon dioxide adsorption and desorption unit, a gas-liquid separation unit and a carbon dioxide liquefaction and storage unit, wherein the waste heat recovery and utilization unit is used for recovering heat in hot air discharged by a data center, and is used for producing steam; the carbon dioxide adsorption and desorption unit is used for separating and capturing carbon dioxide; the gas-liquid separation unit is used for separating water vapor from carbon dioxide; and the carbon dioxide liquefaction and storage unit is used for the compression and storage of carbon dioxide. By combining DAC and data center residual heat air recovery, heat energy and a wind pressure which are discharged by a data center are used for perfectly solving the problems of air induction energy consumption and desorption which are required by the DAC, not only implementing the recovery and utilization of residual heat of the data center, but also capturing carbon dioxide in air on this basis, thereby achieving the aim of negative carbon.

WO2026000592A1.title: DISPOSITIF DE SÉCHAGE DE FEUILLE D'ÉLECTRODE ET APPAREIL DE PRODUCTION DE BATTERIE
WO2026000592A1.abstract: The present application relates to an electrode-sheet drying device and a battery production apparatus. The electrode-sheet drying device comprises: a heating assembly, which comprises a heat-insulating cover and a plurality of heating units arranged at intervals in the heat-insulating cover, all the heating units being used for heating an electrode sheet, and the heating assembly further comprising air extraction pipes, which are in communication with a cavity formed by the heat-insulating cover so as to extract water vapor from the cavity; and a conveying mechanism, which comprises a conveying member for movably passing through the heat-insulating cover, wherein the conveying member is used for carrying and conveying the electrode sheet, so as to drive the electrode sheet to enter or leave the heat-insulating cover, a vacuum cavity is formed inside the conveying member, and suction holes in communication with the vacuum cavity are provided in the surface of the conveying member. In the electrode-sheet drying device of the present application, by means of the coordinated configurations of the heating assembly and the conveying mechanism, the electrode sheet and the conveying member enter the heat-insulating cover while maintaining a state of relative stillness, thereby reducing the probability of the electrode sheet slipping and deviating relative to the conveying member in the heat-insulating cover, and enabling moisture on the surface of the electrode sheet to more efficiently evaporate, thus improving the effect of drying the electrode sheet.

CN121191989A.title: Bump packaging structure and packaging method
CN121191989A.abstract: The invention discloses a bump packaging structure and a packaging method. The packaging method comprises the following steps: step 1, sputtering a seed layer on the surface of a wafer; 2, manufacturing a photoresist opening in the surface of the wafer; 3, at least two metal layers are manufactured in the photoresist opening, so that the Pad is connected out; 4, removing the seed layer on the surface of the wafer to enable the salient points to be directly independent, and avoiding short circuit; and step 5, completing coating, grinding and cutting of the metal layer structure on the surface of the salient point, and manufacturing the whole wafer into a single packaging chip. According to the invention, a compact nickel-gold layer can be formed on the surface of the salient point, corrosion of water vapor to copper is prevented, galvanic effect in a chip cutting process is avoided, the reliability passing rate of the chip in a high-humidity environment is improved, and the preparation method is simple, convenient and suitable for industrial popularization and application.

WO2026006823A1.title: HYDROGELS AUTO-IRRIGUANTS À LIBÉRATION LENTE D'ENGRAIS POUR L'AGRICULTURE DURABLE
WO2026006823A1.abstract: Given the critical need for more efficient water and nutrient utilization to optimize crop yields, effective strategies in water and nutrient management are essential. The development of a self-irrigation and slow-release fertilizer hydrogel (SISRH) represents a promising approach. SISRH, a hydrogel with hygroscopic polymer chains interpenetrated by a thermo-responsive network, enhances plant growth through controlled yet self-sustained water and nutrient delivery. The hydrogel demonstrates diurnal functionality: it absorbs water vapor at night and releases it during the day due to a phase transition in the polymer, with incorporated calcium chloride (CaCl2) further improving hygroscopic properties and controlled nutrient release. SISRH facilitates a slow-release of nutrients and can achieve water savings, significantly reducing the need for frequent irrigation and ensuring robust crop development.

CN121178125A.abstract: According to the MFI-grafted MOR molecular sieve, the preparation method and the application thereof, the MFI-grafted MOR molecular sieve is prepared through step-by-step crystallization and cyclic macromolecular alcohol guidance, the external hydrophobic MFI molecular sieve preferentially adsorbs carbon dioxide, the internal hydrophilic MOR zeolite adsorbs water, and the MFI-grafted MOR molecular sieve has unique hydrophilic/hydrophobic zoning properties. MA68437A1.title: PROCÉDÉ DE COLLECTE D'ÉNERGIE LATENTE ET D'EAU
MA68437A1.abstract: In one embodiment, the invention relates to a method (236) for collecting thermal energy and water from air, the method comprising: receiving a stream of water vapor containing air on a first heat exchange contactor contained in a chamber in a non-sealed state, the first heat exchange contactor being coated with an adsorbent material that adsorbs water vapor (238); desorbing the water vapor from a second heat exchange contactor coated with adsorbent contained in a chamber in a sealed state under partial vacuum (240); exchanging thermal energy between the first heat exchange contactor and the second heat exchange contactor, the heat obtained from the adsorption heat in the first heat exchange contactor being transferred to heat the second heat exchange contactor to facilitate desorption, the waste heat due to the desorption heat in the second heat exchange contactor being transferred to cool the first heat exchange contactor to assist adsorption (242); drawing a vacuum in the sealed chamber to extract air and desorb, compress, and heat water vapor (244); condensing the water vapor in a partial vacuum condenser (246); recovering condensation heat and liquid condensate from water vapor in the condenser (248); and repeating the process with the first heat exchange contactor used for desorption in a sealed state and the second heat exchange contactor used for adsorption in an unsealed state (250).

WO2026004152A1.abstract: The present invention comprises: a light source that generates laser light; a transmission/reception unit that selectively transmits laser light in a first direction, a second direction, and a third direction in the air and receives scattered light; and a calculation unit that calculates the wind direction, the wind speed, and the amount of water vapor in the air on the basis of interference light between the laser light and the scattered light. CN121183565A.title: Green and efficient activation method of graphite felt electrode for all-vanadium redox flow battery
CN121183565A.abstract: The invention belongs to the technical field of flow batteries, and particularly relates to a green and efficient activation method of a graphite felt electrode for an all-vanadium flow battery. Firstly, a graphite felt electrode is placed in an ozone environment to be heated for pre-activation treatment, so that the surface of the graphite felt electrode is provided with oxygen-containing groups, the surface affinity is improved, and the subsequent activation temperature is reduced; a graphite felt pre-activated by ozone is placed in a tubular furnace, a certain amount of water vapor is introduced when the temperature is raised to a certain temperature in a nitrogen atmosphere, so that graphite felt fibers are etched and activated at a high temperature, oxygen-containing functional groups are anchored, defects are increased, and the specific surface area and reaction activity of the electrode are improved. And when the material is applied to the all-vanadium redox flow battery, the energy efficiency of the battery can be effectively improved. The method is simple, green in process, high in practicability and suitable for large-scale production.

CN121182042A.title: Polyolefin resin composition as well as preparation method and application thereof
CN121182042A.abstract: The invention discloses a polyolefin resin composition as well as a preparation method and application thereof. The polyolefin resin composition comprises the following components in parts by weight: 30-70 parts of polyolefin resin; 3 to 10 parts of a polyolefin maleic anhydride graft; 60 to 120 parts of a halogen-free flame retardant; 4 to 15 parts of zinc hydroxystannate; and 4-15 parts of hollow glass beads. The zinc hydroxystannate in the polyolefin resin composition has more hydroxyl groups, and can be decomposed to generate water vapor to take away a large amount of heat when being heated, so that the flame retardant property of the material can be improved; meanwhile, after being matched with the hollow glass beads, the flame retardant can achieve a synergistic flame-retardant effect with resin and a flame retardant, so that the anti-dripping capability is improved. The hollow glass beads are easier to soften at a high temperature and can form carbon at a lower temperature, so that the flame-retardant effect is achieved in a shorter time, the heat release peak value is effectively reduced, the emergency window period is prolonged, and salt mist and toxic gas are reduced.

CN223498717U.title: Emergency cut-off stop valve for liquid hydrogen
CN223498717U.abstract: The utility model provides an emergency cut-off stop valve for liquid hydrogen, which comprises a valve body, a valve clack, a fixer and a sealing ring, and the valve clack is arranged in the valve body; the valve clack is suitable for abutting against the side wall of a runner opening in the valve body. The fixer is connected with the bottom of the valve clack; the sealing ring is arranged between the valve clack and the fixator and is configured to protrude out of the circumferential side of the valve clack, and the part, protruding out of the circumferential side of the valve clack, of the sealing ring is suitable for abutting against the side wall of a runner opening in the valve body and sealing the runner opening; the side wall depth of a flow channel opening in the valve body is larger than the width of the sealing ring protruding out of the circumferential side of the valve clack. The depth of the side wall of the flow channel opening in the valve body is larger than the width of the part, protruding out of the circumferential side of the valve clack, of the sealing ring, after the sealing ring loses efficacy due to excessive abrasion, effective sealing can still be formed through tight attaching between the valve clack and the side wall of the flow channel opening in the valve body, and liquid hydrogen medium leakage is prevented.

CN223404691U.title: Methane hydrogen regeneration olefin recovery system
CN223404691U.abstract: The system is characterized in that the input end of a methane hydrogen heater is communicated with a demethanizer, and the output end of the methane hydrogen heater is communicated with the input end of a liquid phase dryer; the output end of the mixed C4 heater is communicated between the output end of the methane hydrogen heater and the input end of the liquid phase dryer; the output end of the gas-liquid separation tank is communicated with the input end of the regenerated methane hydrogen coalescer; and the output end of the regenerated methane hydrogen coalescer is communicated with the input end of an external fuel pipe network. According to the utility model, a mixed C4 steam stripping process and a regenerated methane hydrogen secondary dehydration process are added, so that the water content in regenerated methane hydrogen gas is ensured to meet the requirements of receiving conditions of a fuel gas pipe network. According to the system for regenerating and recycling olefin by using methane hydrogen, disclosed by the invention, a mixed C4 and methane hydrogen medium is self-produced, so that the purpose of regenerating and recycling ethylene-propylene olefin substances by using the liquid-phase dryer is achieved, the nitrogen consumption and the torch gas emission are reduced, and the ethylene-propylene yield and the resource utilization rate are improved.

CN120717458A.abstract: The invention relates to the technical field of graphene preparation, and provides a graphene preparation system and method.The system comprises a gas supply device, a plasma synthesis device, a gas-solid separation device, a product collection device, a tail gas separation device and a hydrogen storage device. According to the technical scheme, the production cost is reduced, the material handling capacity is large, the productivity is high, the method is suitable for industrial application, energy consumption is low, pollution is avoided, expandability is high, and the application range is wide.

CN223178624U.title: Normal-pressure storage tank for storing liquid hydrogen
CN223178624U.abstract: The utility model provides a normal-pressure storage tank for storing liquid hydrogen. The normal-pressure storage tank comprises an inner tank made of metal and used for containing a low-temperature liquid hydrogen medium, an outer tank located outside the inner tank and made of metal, a heat insulation interlayer located in an interlayer between the inner tank and the outer tank and playing a heat insulation role, and a cold shield arranged in the heat insulation interlayer. The cold shield comprises a cold shield coil pipe which is made of a metal material and is coiled in the heat insulation interlayer, the inlet end of the cold shield coil pipe is connected with a boil-off gas outlet of the gas phase space at the top of the inner tank, and the outlet end of the cold shield coil pipe penetrates through the outer tank, extends out of the outer tank and is connected with a boil-off gas recovery device. The cold shield medium entering the cold shield is liquid hydrogen evaporated gas in the inner tank, the defect of high daily evaporation rate of a liquid hydrogen low-temperature normal-pressure storage technology can be overcome without introducing an external cold source, and the operation cost of the normal-pressure storage tank is reduced.

CN120158600A.abstract: The invention belongs to the technical field of plate and strip production and equipment, and particularly relates to a structure and method for improving air tightness of a rapid cooling section of a continuous annealing furnace in a high-hydrogen mode, the structure comprises a sealing roller chamber shell, a roller and sealing roller chamber shell lining refractory bricks, the bottom of the roller is in sealing connection with a graphite sealing gasket, and the bottom and the two ends of the graphite sealing gasket are fixed through castable; back lining plates are arranged between the two ends of the castable and the sealing roller chamber shell lining refractory bricks, the positions between the back lining plates and the sealing roller chamber shell lining refractory bricks are filled with micropore heat insulation plates, and gaps of the upper end, the side wall and the bottom in the sealing roller chamber are filled with ceramic fibers. The invention mainly aims to ensure the stability of the sealing roller chamber structure of the rapid cooling section, improve the sealing performance in the roller chamber, prevent excessive hydrogen medium from overflowing to the slow cooling section and the overaging section, save the consumption of hydrogen energy and ensure the safety of a unit.

CN119590552A.title: Storage cabin capable of storing ultralow-temperature liquid hydrogen
CN119590552A.abstract: A storage cabin capable of storing ultralow-temperature liquid hydrogen is provided with a cylindrical inner cabin, the inner cabin is supported and fixed in an outer cabin through a filling layer, the inner cabin and the outer cabin form a filling layer space, and the filling layer space is filled with low-temperature LNG or low-temperature liquid nitrogen. The inner cabin is composed of an inner cabin outer wall and an inner cabin inner wall, the inner cabin outer wall and the inner cabin inner wall form a vacuum layer, and the vacuum layer is filled with perlite and vacuumized. A double-layer compartment is arranged, an insulating layer is laid on an outer compartment, an inner compartment is of a vacuum double-wall heat insulation structure, and the space between the inner compartment and the outer compartment is filled with LNG or liquid nitrogen with the temperature close to that of liquid hydrogen. According to the scheme, the ultralow-temperature liquid state maintenance of-163 DEG C LNG or-196 DEG C liquid ammonia medium is easily realized through a mature reliquefaction system, so that the liquid state maintenance of the ultralow-temperature-253 DEG C liquid hydrogen medium is indirectly ensured, the heat transfer between the liquid hydrogen cabin and the external environment can be greatly reduced, and the technical difficulty and the technical cost of ultralow-temperature storage of the liquid hydrogen are reduced.

CN119909468A.title: Medium filtering method and device based on hydrogen purity instrument
CN119909468A.abstract: The invention discloses a medium filtering method and device based on a hydrogen purity instrument. The method comprises the following steps: connecting a sampling pipe to a source of a medium to be measured so as to conveniently introduce the medium; the other end of the sampling pipe is connected with the filtering device, so that the medium passes through the sampling pipe before entering the filtering device; the filtered oil mist is adsorbed and sampled through a filter element in the filtering device, and the oil mist is separated from the hydrogen medium; oil gathered in the filtering device is introduced into the oil storage tank through the drainage pipe; a pressure sensing module and an automatic oil drain valve are installed at the bottom of the oil storage tank. According to the medium filtering method and device based on the hydrogen purity instrument, on one hand, the measurement accuracy of hydrogen purity parameters is improved, a reliable basis is provided for operators to monitor and maintain the purity of hydrogen, and the operation economy and safety of a generator are guaranteed; on the other hand, the automatic oil discharging valve is adopted to achieve the automatic sewage discharging function, the daily maintenance workload is reduced, and the automation level of equipment is improved.

CN119198066A.title: Safety monitoring method and equipment of vehicle-mounted hydrogen storage system and storage medium
CN119198066A.abstract: The invention discloses a safety monitoring method and equipment of a vehicle-mounted hydrogen storage system and a storage medium, the vehicle-mounted hydrogen storage system comprises a hydrogen in-pile stop valve, a hydrogen discharge valve, a hydrogen storage bottle, a pressure reducing valve and a hydrogen medium-pressure sensor, and the hydrogen medium-pressure sensor, the pressure reducing valve and the hydrogen in-pile stop valve are sequentially arranged on a pipeline between the hydrogen storage bottle and a fuel cell stack; the method comprises the following steps: acquiring a first opening and closing state of a hydrogen inlet pile stop valve, a second opening and closing state of the hydrogen discharge valve and medium-pressure pressure detected by a hydrogen medium-pressure sensor; determining a fault level threshold according to the first opening and closing state and the second opening and closing state; and determining the fault level of the medium-pressure pressure according to the medium-pressure pressure and the fault level threshold. The fault level is judged by combining the opening and closing states of the hydrogen reactor inlet stop valve and the hydrogen discharge valve, so that the misjudgment and false alarm of the medium-pressure pressure fault under different operation conditions can be effectively avoided, and the accuracy of safety monitoring is improved.

CN119244528A.title: Liquid hydrogen conveying centrifugal pump
CN119244528A.abstract: The invention discloses a liquid hydrogen conveying centrifugal pump which comprises a rotor assembly, an inner shell assembly and an outer shell assembly. The rotor assembly is sleeved in the inner shell assembly to form a core; and the core is sleeved in the outer shell assembly. A multi-stage centrifugal pump structure is adopted for conveying a liquid hydrogen medium, and a three-stage centrifugal wheel with long blades, middle blades and short blades, a radial inlet annular inlet shell, a combined inter-stage overflowing structure with radial guide blades and reverse guide blades and a combined sealing structure with static sealing, end face sealing and blow-off isolation sealing are adopted. By adopting a mounting mode which is compact in structure and convenient to disassemble, the liquid hydrogen medium can be transferred from an offshore transport ship to a storage tank, from the storage tank to the storage tank, from the storage tank to a tank car and the like; the whole structure is simple, and disassembly, assembly, disassembly and replacement are convenient; and the technical blank is filled.

CN119170829A.abstract: The invention relates to the technical field of fuel cell control, and particularly provides a fuel cell hydrogen medium pressure control method, system and device.The method comprises the steps that a current hydrogen medium pressure fluctuation value of a hydrogen system in the hydrogen discharging process is obtained; and under the condition that the current hydrogen medium pressure fluctuation value does not meet the preset condition, the hydrogen supply hydrogen pressure in the hydrogen system is controlled. CN118463032A.abstract: The invention discloses a fast-response liquid hydrogen storage and supply system and method, and relates to the technical field of hydrogen-air fuel cells. CN119468049A.title: High-temperature solid hydrogen storage and SOEC coupling hydrogen production and storage integrated system and method
CN119468049A.abstract: The invention discloses a high-temperature solid hydrogen storage and SOEC coupling hydrogen production and storage integrated system and method. CN119062897A.title: Normal pressure storage tank for storing liquid hydrogen
CN119062897A.abstract: The invention provides a normal pressure storage tank for liquid hydrogen storage. The normal pressure storage tank comprises an inner tank made of metal and used for containing a low-temperature liquid hydrogen medium, an outer tank located outside the inner tank and made of metal, a heat insulation interlayer located in an interlayer between the inner tank and the outer tank and playing a heat insulation role, and a cold shield arranged in the heat insulation interlayer. The cold shield comprises a cold shield coil pipe which is made of a metal material and is coiled in the heat insulation interlayer, the inlet end of the cold shield coil pipe is connected with a boil-off gas outlet of the gas phase space at the top of the inner tank, and the outlet end of the cold shield coil pipe penetrates through the outer tank, extends out of the outer tank and is connected with a boil-off gas recovery device. The cold shield medium entering the cold shield is the liquid hydrogen evaporation gas in the inner tank, the defect that the daily evaporation rate of the liquid hydrogen low-temperature normal-pressure storage technology is high can be overcome without introducing an external cold source, and the operation cost of the normal-pressure storage tank is reduced.

CN118391579A.abstract: The invention discloses a liquid hydrogen vaporization system and method, and relates to the technical field of liquid hydrogen. The system comprises a liquid hydrogen pressurization pipeline, a liquid hydrogen tank, a liquid hydrogen supply pipeline, a flat vaporizer, an air pipeline, a water pipeline and a liquid carbon dioxide pipeline. KR20240105888A.abstract: An object of the present invention is to provide an electrolyte for a secondary battery capable of improving high-temperature storage characteristics and high-temperature lifespan characteristics of a lithium secondary battery, and a secondary battery including the same. The electrolyte for a lithium secondary battery of the present invention comprises: a lithium salt; an organic solvent; and a sulfonyl-based compound represented by chemical formula 1. In the chemical formula 1, R1 and R2 are independently selected from hydrogen, medium and small hydrogen, and a substituted or non-substituted C1-C4 alkyl group. CN117965857A.abstract: The invention discloses a high-strength pipeline steel plate suitable for a hydrogen conveying pipeline and a manufacturing method, and belongs to the technical field of metal materials. CN117865069A.abstract: The invention relates to a hydrogen purification method for removing carbon monoxide by using a molecular sieve, which adopts a purification device to carry out carbon monoxide removal on input hydrogen containing carbon monoxide. CN220268467U.abstract: The liquid hydrogen low-temperature stop valve particularly relates to the field of stop valves and comprises a main body mechanism, the main body mechanism comprises a valve body, a first through groove is formed in the valve body, second through grooves communicated with the first through groove are formed in the two sides of the first through groove, and a valve element is inserted into the first through groove in an up-down sliding state. A valve rod is fixedly installed at the top of the valve element, the valve rod can be arranged in the first through groove in a reciprocating motion state in the axial direction of the valve rod, a valve body lengthening pipe is fixedly installed at the top of the valve body, and the outer wall of the valve rod is sleeved with the valve body lengthening pipe. The valve body extension pipe is arranged on the outer wall of the valve rod, and the guide heat insulation ring is arranged between the valve rod and the valve body extension pipe, so that heat convection of a liquid hydrogen medium in the valve is reduced, liquid hydrogen cold energy is prevented from being conducted to other sealing parts of the sealing corrugated pipe fitting, the sealing performance and the service life of the valve are guaranteed, and the service life of the valve is prolonged. And the liquid hydrogen medium storage and transportation safety can be improved.

CN219930270U.title: Electrolytic bath flushing device
CN219930270U.abstract: The utility model belongs to the technical field of hydrogen production through alkaline water electrolysis, and discloses an electrolytic bath flushing device. According to the electrolytic bath flushing device, through cooperation of the circulating pump, the connecting pipeline and the valves, cleaning liquid is pumped out of the electrolytic bath from the alkali inlet in the bottom of the electrolytic bath and injected into the electrolytic bath again through the hydrogen medium outlet or the oxygen medium outlet to conduct forward cleaning on the electrolytic bath, or through cooperation of the liquid storage tank, the circulating pump, the connecting pipeline and the valves, the electrolytic bath is flushed. Cleaning liquid in the liquid storage box is injected from the alkali inlet in the bottom of the electrolytic bath and pumped out through the hydrogen medium outlet and the oxygen medium outlet in the electrolytic bath to conduct reverse cleaning on the electrolytic bath, the electrolytic bath can be subjected to forward flushing and back flushing through the electrolytic bath flushing device, welding slag, nickel slag, dust and other impurities in the electrolytic bath can be efficiently removed, and the electrolytic bath can be cleaned more efficiently. The service life of the electrolytic bath is effectively prolonged.

CN117288397A.abstract: The invention discloses a liquid hydrogen valve sealing performance testing device, and relates to the technical field of ultralow temperature valve performance testing, the liquid hydrogen valve sealing performance testing device comprises a hydrogen supply subsystem, a hydrogen cooling and liquefying subsystem and a valve sealing performance detection subsystem, and an internal cooling method can be adopted to carry out internal leakage detection and external leakage detection on a tested valve in a liquid hydrogen environment; the hydrogen replacement and recovery subsystem switches a gas purging replacement pipeline and a hydrogen recovery pipeline by controlling the flow direction of a cut-off reversing mechanism, and hydrogen which is not liquefied in the pre-cooling stage can be recovered and reused. CN219673355U.title: Ball valve
CN219673355U.abstract: The utility model discloses a ball valve which comprises a valve cover, a valve body, a cut-off structure and a blocking structure. Wherein a circulation channel is formed in the valve body; the cut-off structure is mounted in the valve body, and the cut-off structure is connected with the circulation channel so as to open or close the circulation channel; the blocking structure is formed between the valve body and the valve deck, and the blocking structure, the valve body and the valve deck jointly define an isolation cavity. The ball valve has the transportation state that the low-temperature medium is isolated in the circulation channel through the blocking structure. According to the structure, when a low-temperature medium exists in the circulation channel, the low-temperature medium is blocked in the circulation channel, so that the low-temperature medium cannot be close to the sealing piece, and the flange of the valve cover of the valve body is sealed to be far away from the low-temperature liquid hydrogen medium, so that the sealing piece cannot be located in an ultralow-temperature environment for a long time, deformation caused by the ultralow temperature cannot occur, and the service life of the sealing piece is prolonged. Therefore, the service life of the sealing piece is prolonged, and leakage is prevented.

CN116825257A.title: Method and system for predicting fatigue characteristic of hydrogen storage bottle
CN116825257A.abstract: The invention discloses a hydrogen storage bottle fatigue characteristic prediction method and system. The prediction method comprises the following steps: carrying out a hydrogen charging and discharging thermodynamic test on the hydrogen storage cylinder under a hydrogen medium condition; establishing a hydrogen storage bottle thermodynamic model; testing thermal stress of the hydrogen storage cylinder; establishing a hydrogen storage cylinder thermal stress finite element simulation model; acquiring characteristics and failure behaviors of a fiber material, characteristics and failure behaviors of a matrix material, characteristics and failure behaviors of a fiber-matrix interface material and interlayer debonding characteristics of a composite material at different temperatures; establishing a composite material microscopic equivalent volume element finite element model; carrying out fatigue failure test on the laminated plate at different temperatures; establishing a fatigue characteristic finite element model of the multi-scale composite laminated plate under thermal-mechanical coupling; establishing a multi-scale multi-failure mode fatigue characteristic finite element model of the hydrogen storage cylinder under hydrogen thermal coupling; performing fatigue test on the hydrogen storage cylinder; and predicting the fatigue characteristic of the hydrogen storage cylinder. According to the method, the fatigue failure behavior of the hydrogen storage cylinder under the hydrogen-heat-force coupling condition can be accurately predicted.

CN116624752A.abstract: The invention discloses a low-evaporation-rate liquid hydrogen storage and supply system which comprises a first power generation type hydrogen heat exchanger, a second power generation type hydrogen heat exchanger and a liquid hydrogen storage tank, and a hydrogen liquefaction unit and a liquid hydrogen pressurization unit are installed on the liquid hydrogen storage tank. CN115013720A.title: Methanol hydrogen production and refueling station
CN115013720A.abstract: The station comprises a low-temperature methanol storage tank, an oxygen storage tank, a water tank, a methanol comprehensive heat exchange module, a methanol hydrogen production device, a hydrogen purification device, a hydrogen buffer tank, a hydrogen storage compressor, a hydrogen low-pressure storage tank, a hydrogenation compressor, a hydrogen medium-pressure storage tank, a 35MPa hydrogenation machine, a high-pressure hydrogenation compressor, a hydrogen high-pressure storage tank and a 70MPa hydrogenation machine. The methanol hydrogen production and refueling station has the advantages that (1) the methanol hydrogen production and refueling station can realize on-site hydrogen production of the refueling station, the hydrogen production cost is relatively low, the problem that the layout of the refueling station is influenced by non-uniform distribution of hydrogen sources can be well solved, and the transportation cost is saved; (2) the methanol is in a liquid state at normal temperature and normal pressure, the energy density is high, the storage conditions are consistent, and the requirements on pipelines and valves in the filling process are low; (3) the hydrogen production and hydrogenation cost is low, and the market popularization of hydrogen energy is facilitated; and (4) low-temperature methanol is coupled with a cold energy and hydrogenation system, so that the integration level and the utilization efficiency of the system are improved.

CN115325201A.title: Intelligent high-pressure hydrogen valve
CN115325201A.abstract: The invention relates to the technical field of valves, and discloses an intelligent high-pressure hydrogen valve which comprises a valve body, a center cavity is formed in the valve body, a valve element is rotatably arranged in the center cavity, a valve rod is fixedly installed on the valve element, and the tail end of the valve rod penetrates through a valve sleeve on the valve body and extends out of the valve body. A boss is formed on the inner wall of the valve sleeve, padding is arranged on the boss, the padding is pressed on the boss by a pressing seat, and the pressing seat is detachably connected to the valve sleeve through a connecting bolt. The threaded sleeve is screwed on and can tightly press the absorption ring through the pressing ring, so that the position of the absorption ring is stably fixed, the absorption ring can effectively absorb hydrogen leaked between the pressing seat and the valve rod through the side groove in the inner circumferential wall of the annular groove, and hydrogen leaked between the valve sleeve and the pressing seat can be effectively absorbed through the side groove in the outer circumferential wall of the annular groove. Therefore, the effect of effectively removing leaked hydrogen is achieved, and normal use of the valve under a high-pressure hydrogen medium is guaranteed.

CN115013729A.title: Middle-high pressure gas conveying system and method adopting double-layer gas channel composite material pipe
CN115013729A.abstract: The invention discloses a medium-high pressure gas conveying system adopting double-layer gas channel composite material pipes, which comprises a main pipeline, a low-pressure accompanying pipeline, a low-pressure gas leading-out pipeline and a gas reinjection pipeline, and is characterized in that the main pipeline is formed by splicing a plurality of composite material pipes through connecting fittings; the composite material pipe comprises a multi-layer pipe body, the pipe body at least comprises an outer structural layer and an inner structural layer, and a low-pressure air guide layer is arranged between the outer structural layer and the inner structural layer; the low-pressure gas leading-out pipeline and the gas reinjection pipeline are arranged between the low-pressure accompanying pipeline and the main pipeline; and the low-pressure gas leading-out pipeline, the gas reinjection pipeline and the low-pressure accompanying pipeline form a low-pressure gas loop. By means of product characteristics and active management in system operation, the problem of gas permeation in medium-high pressure gas long-distance pipe transportation is solved, and therefore the problem of gas leakage in hydrogen medium-high pressure long transportation is thoroughly solved. The invention further discloses a medium-high pressure gas conveying method adopting the double-layer gas channel composite pipe.

CN217207753U.title: Combined ultrahigh-pressure hydrogen sealing structure
CN217207753U.abstract: The utility model discloses a combined type ultrahigh pressure hydrogen sealing structure which comprises a sealing structure body, the sealing structure body is composed of a sealing valve rod, a sealing base and a sealing ring, and the sealing ring is arranged between the sealing valve rod and the sealing base. According to the structure, pure hard sealing and soft sealing are replaced in a mode of combining soft contact sealing and hard contact sealing, the defect that the single sealing mode is poor in pressure adaptability is effectively overcome, the requirements for part machining precision and material selection are reduced to a certain degree, and the production efficiency is improved. A brand-new sealing structure is provided for gas sealing involved in the ultrahigh-pressure hydrogen storage and transportation process; hard contact sealing and soft contact sealing are combined on the same sealing structure for use, reliable sealing of an ultrahigh pressure hydrogen medium is achieved, the requirement of pure hard sealing for part machining precision is lowered, the requirement of pure soft sealing for sealing ring materials is also lowered, and the cost of sealing structural parts is greatly reduced.

CN114877140A.title: Liquid hydrogen conveying pipeline
CN114877140A.abstract: The invention discloses a liquid hydrogen conveying pipeline which comprises at least one section of conveying pipeline, the conveying pipeline comprises an inner pipe and an outer pipe, the inner pipe is arranged in the outer pipe and is coaxial with the outer pipe, a vacuum interlayer space is formed between the inner pipe and the outer pipe, a plurality of heat insulation supporting assemblies are further arranged between the inner pipe and the outer pipe, and a heat bridge assembly is arranged at the end of each section of conveying pipeline. The heat bridge assembly is arranged between the inner pipes and the outer pipe, expansion joints are arranged between the inner pipes in a sleeved mode, and a vacuum pumping opening penetrating through the interior of the outer pipe is formed in the outer pipe. A double-layer structure is adopted, a liquid hydrogen medium is conveyed through the inner pipe, vacuum is formed between the inner pipe and the outer pipe, the heat insulation supporting assembly is used for supporting, pipeline rigidity is guaranteed, stable conveying of liquid hydrogen is achieved, heat energy transfer is reduced, and loss in the liquid hydrogen conveying process is reduced.

CN216667249U.title: Low-pressure liquid hydrogen concrete storage tank with non-self-supporting main container
CN216667249U.abstract: The utility model relates to a main container non-self-supporting low-pressure liquid hydrogen concrete storage tank, which comprises a pile foundation, a prestressed concrete outer tank, a tank top, a corrugated structure inner tank, a hard thermal insulation layer and a leakproof two-layer bottom, the prestressed concrete outer tank comprises a bearing platform and a tank wall, the bearing platform is arranged on the pile foundation and is rigidly connected with the pile foundation, and the tank wall is arranged on the tank wall. The bottom end of the tank wall is fixedly connected with the bearing platform, the bearing platform and the tank wall are both made of concrete, and prestressed steel bars are arranged in wall bodies of the bearing platform and the tank wall; the tank top comprises a prestressed concrete dome and a metal suspended ceiling, prestressed steel bars are arranged in the dome, the main container is a non-self-supporting low-pressure liquid hydrogen coagulation storage tank, the design pressure of the storage tank is below 1.6 MPa (G), the effective volume of the storage tank is 0.4-27 thousand cubic meters, and the storage tank is used for storing a liquid hydrogen medium and can also be used for storing an industrial gas low-temperature medium after evaluation. The problem that large-scale low-temperature liquid hydrogen storage is difficult is solved.

GEP19950095B.title: Method for Production of Softener for Rubber from Secondary Oils
GEP19950095B.abstract: This method refers to chemical industry, in particular to methods for production of softeners for manufacturing rubber. Technical result is to provide a simplified method of manufacturing and obtaining toxically pure softeners. The production of the secondary transformer oil is achieved in two steps: first step provides contact purification of oil by means of gumbrine aluminosilicate having high crushing quality, the second step deals with catalysis percent of intermediate product proceeded by preliminary regenerated manganese substituted catalyst in hydrogen medium under a pressure of 0.1-1.0 mpa, at a temperature of 280-290� C during 1.5-2 hours.

CN114544107A.title: Vehicle-mounted liquid hydrogen valve leakage detection device
CN114544107A.abstract: The invention relates to a vehicle-mounted liquid hydrogen valve leakage detection device, and belongs to the field of low-temperature valve leakage detection. The device is composed of a pressurization gas cylinder, a liquid hydrogen container for testing, a filling valve, a discharge valve, a upstream thermometer, a downstream thermometer, a tested valve, a sleeve, a flow detection valve, a digital flow meter, a water bubble meter, a liquid hydrogen recovery valve, a liquid hydrogen pipeline, a liquid hydrogen recovery storage tank, a transfer injection valve, a hydrogen recovery valve, a heater, a recovery press and a hydrogen storage tank. In the leakage detection process, real working conditions are simulated, a liquid hydrogen medium is adopted, the actual leakage amount of valve hydrogen is measured, an accurate and reliable data basis is provided for liquid hydrogen valve quality identification, meanwhile, the testing device recycles liquid hydrogen and hydrogen, and energy can be effectively saved, and consumption can be effectively reduced.

CN113847494A.title: Rotary joint special for ultralow-temperature liquid hydrogen
CN113847494A.abstract: The invention relates to the technical field of ultralow-temperature fluid medium corollary equipment, in particular to a rotary joint special for ultralow-temperature liquid hydrogen. The rotary joint comprises an inner ring, an outer ring and a flange. According to the rotary joint special for ultralow-temperature liquid hydrogen, the structure is designed into a double-layer structure, the interior of the rotary joint is provided with a liquid hydrogen medium channel, an interlayer is vacuumized, inert gas is introduced into a gap between roller paths, and the energy transfer area is reduced through the jacket vacuumizing structure; the inner ring of the rotary joint is designed into a jacket structure, so that the roller paths and steel balls are separated from an internal ultralow-temperature medium through a jacket space, the energy transfer area is reduced, the influence of the low-temperature medium in the joint on the roller paths is reduced, and the influence of low-temperature shrinkage borne by a rotary shaft is greatly reduced; and the rotary joint is designed to be of a jacket structure, a jacket space is subjected to vacuumizing, inert gas is introduced into the gap of the roller paths for replacement, the content of water in the working environment of the rotary joint is reduced, and it is ensured that the roller paths of the joint cannot be frozen.
