CN120171276A.abstract: The invention relates to the technical field of liquid hydrogen storage and supply systems, and discloses an instrument valve box assembly for a vehicle-mounted liquid hydrogen system, and the instrument valve box assembly for the vehicle-mounted liquid hydrogen system comprises a box body which is provided with a gas inlet main pipe, and the gas inlet main pipe is suitable for being communicated with a liquid hydrogen container; the pipeline system is arranged in the box body, the pipeline system comprises a plurality of pipelines which are compactly arranged, and the plurality of pipelines are respectively communicated with the air inlet main pipe; and the explosion-proof box is fixedly connected with the outer wall of the box body, and the interior of the explosion-proof box is suitable for installing a control communication module. According to the instrument valve box assembly for the vehicle-mounted liquid hydrogen system, the multiple pipelines communicate with the liquid hydrogen container through the gas inlet header pipe, so that the number of connecting points of a pipeline system and the liquid hydrogen container is reduced, and the convenience of disassembly, assembly and maintenance is improved; and the control communication module is wrapped with the explosion-proof box, so that the control communication module is separated from the hydrogen environment, and the potential safety hazard of explosion in the low-temperature and vibration environment is reduced.

CN120142001A.title: Method for obtaining critical stress intensity factor of hydrogen environment material
CN120142001A.abstract: The invention discloses a method for obtaining a critical stress intensity factor of a hydrogen environment material, and belongs to the technical field of stress intensity factor measurement. According to the method disclosed by the invention, a hollow notched sample is prepared from a suitable pipeline material according to hydrogen embrittlement research, then the two ends of the sample are filled with hydrogen, tensile test loading is carried out, and the test is stopped after the sample is failed and fractured due to hydrogen embrittlement. The method is more in line with the actual stress state of the pipeline, can provide the material stress intensity factor value and the critical fracture stress intensity factor of the material under different loads, and has important significance for evaluating the fracture toughness and crack resistance of the material in the hydrogen environment.

CN120138483A.title: Hydrogen embrittlement precipitation resistant strengthened high-nickel austenitic stainless steel and preparation method thereof
CN120138483A.abstract: The invention relates to the technical field of preparation of novel hydrogen embrittlement-resistant materials of high-strength austenitic stainless steel, in particular to hydrogen embrittlement precipitation-resistant reinforced high-nickel austenitic stainless steel and a preparation method thereof. The high-nickel austenitic stainless steel is subjected to high-temperature solution treatment, so that solute elements of the high-nickel austenitic stainless steel are uniformly dissolved in an austenite matrix, then rolling treatment is performed, and a uniform and fine austenite structure is obtained; then, medium and low temperature tempering treatment is carried out to obtain a uniformly-distributed double-phase structure, and a high-temperature medium-rate rolling deformation process is adopted to obtain an ultra-fine grain structure; and finally, a nano precipitated phase is formed under the low-temperature condition, the strength of an austenite structure is improved, the hydrogen embrittlement resistance is enhanced, the austenite structure is not coarsened after welding, and therefore the use requirement in the low-temperature hydrogen environment with the high pressure of 70 Mpa is met. The problem that austenitic stainless steel in the prior art is poor in hydrogen embrittlement resistance and cannot meet application in a high-pressure and low-temperature hydrogen environment is solved.

CN120026357A.abstract: The invention discloses a gamma-manganese oxide doped carbon layer coated Pt and Co catalyst as well as a preparation method and application thereof, and belongs to the technical field of biomass resource utilization. CN120012464A.title: Phase field simulation method, device and equipment for simulating hydrogen-induced fatigue crack propagation of material and storage medium
CN120012464A.abstract: The invention relates to the technical field of computational material science, and discloses a phase field simulation method, device and equipment for simulating hydrogen-induced fatigue crack propagation of a material and a storage medium. The method comprises the following steps: acquiring hydrogen transmission parameters and mechanical property parameters of a target material, and establishing a material deformation equation, a hydrogen diffusion equation and a phase field evolution equation corresponding to the target material based on the hydrogen transmission parameters and the mechanical property parameters; constructing a three-field coupling relation by using a material deformation equation, a hydrogen diffusion equation and a phase field evolution equation to obtain a three-field coupling phase field simulation model; and building boundary conditions of a hydrogen diffusion equation based on a surface hydrogen absorption flux equation corresponding to the target material, and solving the three-field coupling phase field simulation model to obtain crack propagation parameters of the target material in the gaseous hydrogen environment. Therefore, through the constructed three-field coupling phase field simulation model, the dynamic crack propagation behavior of the material in the gaseous hydrogen environment is accurately described, and the prediction precision of the crack propagation process of the material in the complex environment is remarkably improved.

CN119915811A.title: System and method for verifying effectiveness of hydrogen-sensitive adhesive tape
CN119915811A.abstract: The invention discloses a hydrogen-sensitive adhesive tape effectiveness verification system and method. Comprising a high-low temperature environment bin, a hydrogen environment bin is arranged in the high-low temperature environment bin, a transparent observation opening is formed in the hydrogen environment bin, a supporting device is arranged in the hydrogen environment bin, a standard gas cylinder is communicated with the hydrogen environment bin through a gas inlet pipe, and a gas outlet pipe is arranged on the side wall of the hydrogen environment bin; an inlet valve is arranged on the air inlet pipe, and an outlet valve is arranged on the air outlet pipe; a first hydrogen concentration sensor, a pressure sensor and a humidity sensor are arranged on the side wall of the hydrogen environment cabin, and a humidifier is arranged in the hydrogen environment cabin; a video shooting device is arranged in the high and low temperature environment bin; the first hydrogen concentration sensor, the pressure sensor, the humidity sensor, the humidifier and the video shooting device are all connected with a control system; the vacuum pump is connected with the hydrogen environment bin through an exhaust pipe. The system provided by the invention can verify the hydrogen leakage detection reliability of the hydrogen-sensitive adhesive tape, explores the color change performance of the hydrogen-sensitive adhesive tape under different environmental conditions, and is simple and convenient to operate.

CN119923487A.title: Steel pipe for line pipe having excellent hydrogen embrittlement resistance, method for producing same, steel material for line pipe, and method for producing same
CN119923487A.abstract: The purpose of the present invention is to provide natural gas suitable as 100% hydrogen or hydrogen having a hydrogen partial pressure of 1 MPa or more (natural gas being gas mainly composed of hydrocarbons such as methane or ethane) A steel pipe for a line pipe having excellent hydrogen embrittlement resistance in a high-pressure hydrogen environment and having high strength for a steel structure used in a high-pressure hydrogen environment, such as a line pipe; a method for manufacturing the steel pipe; a steel material for a line pipe; and a method for manufacturing the steel material for a line pipe. A steel pipe for a line pipe having excellent hydrogen embrittlement resistance, characterized by having a specific component composition and a specific structure, having a fatigue limit stress in hydrogen at 1 MPa or more of 200 MPa or more, and having a fatigue limit stress in hydrogen at 1 MPa or more/fatigue limit stress in an inert gas atmosphere of 0.90 or more.

CN119915614A.title: Mobile assembly and disassembly type high-temperature and high-pressure hydrogen environment in-situ tensile test device and method
CN119915614A.abstract: The invention provides a mobile assembly and disassembly type high-temperature and high-pressure hydrogen environment in-situ tensile test device and method, the device is suitable for a universal testing machine, and the device comprises a mobile base frame, a power supporting assembly and an assembly and disassembly type tensile kettle. The base frame is provided with a gravity balance plate and a guide rail, and the supporting frame sliding block slides on the guide rail and is convenient to move. The power supporting assembly comprises a hollow supporting stand column, a lifting sleeve, a mounting arm and a lifting supporting plate, and lifting and rotating of the stretching kettle are achieved. The stretching kettle is composed of a kettle body, a kettle cover and a stretching shaft and can fix a stretching sample, and high-pressure hydrogen is prevented from leaking in a combined sealing mode. The method comprises the steps of sample installation, gas inflation and deflation, heating, alignment, tensile test, data storage, cooling disassembly and the like. The problems that a traditional device is poor in flexibility, limited in performance, inaccurate in strain measurement and the like are solved, the testing efficiency and applicability are improved, and the long-term mechanical behavior of the metal sealing layer in the high-pressure and high-temperature hydrogen environment can be explored easily.

CN119824291A.title: High-pressure-resistant hydrogen pipeline steel as well as preparation method and application thereof
CN119824291A.abstract: The invention relates to the technical field of pipeline steel production, and provides high-pressure-resistant hydrogen pipeline steel as well as a preparation method and application thereof.The high-pressure-resistant hydrogen pipeline steel comprises the following chemical components in percentage by mass: 0.02%-0.05% of C, 0.15%-0.45% of Si, 1.0%-1.4% of Mn, 0.15%-0.4% of Ni, 0.1%-0.4% of Mo, 0.03%-0.06% of Nb, 0.01%-0.04% of Ti, 0.04%-0.15% of V, less than or equal to 0.015% of P, less than or equal to 0.005% of S and the balance of Fe and other inevitable impurities. The method comprises the following steps: smelting and casting into a blank; heating the casting blank; two-stage rolling; cooling is performed; performing heat preservation treatment. According to the patent technology, the yield strength ranges from 510 MPa to 530 MPa, the tensile strength ranges from 570 MPa to 630 MPa, the ductility under the 10 MPa hydrogen environment is larger than 20%, and the hydrogen embrittlement sensitivity is smaller than 17%.

CN119869514A.title: Ruthenium monatomic alloy catalyst and plastic hydrogenolysis method
CN119869514A.abstract: The invention provides a ruthenium monatomic alloy catalyst and a plastic hydrogenolysis method. The ruthenium monatomic alloy catalyst comprises ruthenium monatomic alloy and a carrier, the general formula of the ruthenium monatomic alloy is RuSAM, M is selected from one or a combination of more than two of Pt, Pd, Fe, Co, Ni, Cu, Zn, Mg, Ca and Mn; wherein the value of SA ranges from 0.01 to 1. The invention also provides a plastic hydrogenolysis method which is carried out by adopting the ruthenium monatomic alloy catalyst. The plastic hydrogenolysis method comprises the following steps: mixing the plastic with the ruthenium monatomic alloy catalyst, and carrying out hydrogenolysis in a hydrogen environment. According to the ruthenium monatomic alloy catalyst and the application method in plastic hydrogenolysis, plastic can be efficiently and catalytically converted into high-value liquid fuel and lubricating oil base oil under the conditions of wide hydrogen pressure range and low temperature.

CN119876785A.title: High-grade hydrogen transmission pipeline steel and preparation method thereof
CN119876785A.abstract: The invention belongs to the technical field of ferrous metallurgy, and relates to high-grade hydrogen transmission pipeline steel and a preparation method thereof.The high-grade hydrogen transmission pipeline steel is prepared from, by mass, 0.02%-0.04% of C, 0.20%-0.30% of Si, 1.20%-1.30% of Mn, smaller than or equal to 0.010% of P, smaller than or equal to 0.0010% of S, 0.015%-0.055% of Alt, smaller than or equal to 0.0020% of N, smaller than or equal to 0.0010% of O, smaller than or equal to 0.0001% of H, 0.90%-1.00% of Cr, 0.40%-0.50% of Mo, 0.020%-0.030% of Ti, 0.05%-0.07% of Nb, 0.40%-0.50% of Ni and the balance iron and inevitable impurities; various performance indexes, especially hydrogen induced crack resistance indexes, of the composite material are outstanding, CSR is equal to 0, CLR is equal to 0, CTR is equal to 0, slow tensile IRA and IEL are smaller than or equal to 20% in a solution A, KIH of a stepped compact tensile sample is larger than or equal to 100 MPa.m < 1/2 > in a 6.3 MPa pure hydrogen environment, and the fatigue life is larger than or equal to 500,000 times; according to the method, the technical problems in the research and development process of the steel material for the hydrogen conveying pipeline are solved, the technical scheme is relatively simple and easy to implement, the obtained product is high in performance stability and controllable in cost, and popularization and application in long-distance, large-scale and safe conveying pipeline construction of high-pressure hydrogen are facilitated.

CN119909606A.title: Oxygen carrier lifting circulation system for hydrogen production through chemical looping reaction
CN119909606A.abstract: The invention relates to an oxygen carrier lifting circulation system for hydrogen production through chemical looping reaction. The oxygen carrier lifting circulation system comprises an oxygen carrier feeding hopper, an oxygen environment reaction unit, a nitrogen sealing tank, a hydrogen environment reaction unit and an oxygen carrier lifting unit which are sequentially arranged. An oxygen carrier flows among the oxygen environment reaction unit, the nitrogen sealing tank and the hydrogen environment reaction unit in sequence from the oxygen carrier feeding hopper through self gravity flow until the oxygen carrier reaches the oxygen carrier lifting unit; the device further comprises a lifting gas circulation unit, the lifting gas circulation unit is used for providing recyclable lifting gas, and the oxygen carrier reaching the oxygen carrier lifting unit is lifted and returned to the oxygen carrier feeding hopper under the driving of the lifting gas; the lifting gas comprises circularly flowing nitrogen, and the nitrogen sealing tank and the lifting gas circulating unit are respectively connected with a nitrogen supplementing unit. According to the invention, switching of the oxygen carrier in different reaction gas environments can be realized, and the oxygen carrier can be stably and reliably circulated in the system.

JP2025054904A.title: METHOD FOR PRODUCING HIGH-PRESSURE HYDROGEN COMPONENT MADE OF MARTENSITIC STAINLESS STEEL
JP2025054904A.abstract: To provide a producing method capable of improving strength of a high pressure hydrogen component almost free from strength decrease under high pressure hydrogen environment, excellent in low temperature toughness, also excellent in corrosion resistance, and capable of saving resource.SOLUTION: A method for producing a high-pressure hydrogen component made of martensitic stainless steel containing, by mass%, C: 0.01-0.05%, Si: 0.70% or less, Mn: 1.10% or less, P: 0.040% or less, S: 0.030% or less, Ni: 4.00-5.70%, Cr: 17.00-20.50%, and as an optional element, Ca: 0.0005-0.0030%, and as an optional element, B: 0.0005-0.0050%, satisfying formula 1: 3.0>Ni-0.8Cr+11.36>0, with the balance being Fe and unavoidable impurities.
